---
title: "Soccer Event Animation & STRAIN Analysis"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Reading in the Data

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

# data from https://github.com/metrica-sports/sample-data/tree/master/data

# Read the data
possession_data <- read.csv("Sample_Game_1_RawEventsData.csv")
tracking_home   <- read_csv("Sample_Game_1_RawTrackingData_Home_Team.csv", skip = 2)
tracking_away   <- read_csv("Sample_Game_1_RawTrackingData_Away_Team.csv", skip = 2)

# Function to rename columns properly
fix_tracking_columns <- function(df) {
  cols     <- colnames(df)
  new_cols <- cols

  for (i in 1:(length(cols) - 1)) {
    if (grepl("^Player\\d+$", cols[i])) {
      new_cols[i]     <- paste0(cols[i], "_x")
      new_cols[i + 1] <- paste0(cols[i], "_y")
    } else if (cols[i] == "Ball") {
      new_cols[i]     <- "Ball_x"
      new_cols[i + 1] <- "Ball_y"
    }
  }

  colnames(df) <- new_cols
  return(df)
}

# Apply the fix
tracking_home <- fix_tracking_columns(tracking_home)
tracking_away <- fix_tracking_columns(tracking_away)

# Reshape to long format
tracking_home_long <- tracking_home %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols         = matches("^Player\\d+_[xy]$"),
    names_to     = c("player", "coord"),
    names_pattern = "(Player\\d+)_([xy])"
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = coord, values_from = value) %>%
  mutate(team = "Home") %>%
  rename(time = `Time [s]`)

tracking_away_long <- tracking_away %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols         = matches("^Player\\d+_[xy]$"),
    names_to     = c("player", "coord"),
    names_pattern = "(Player\\d+)_([xy])"
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = coord, values_from = value) %>%
  mutate(team = "Away") %>%
  rename(time = `Time [s]`)

# Combine and rescale coordinates to centred metres
all_players <- bind_rows(tracking_home_long, tracking_away_long) %>%
  select(-Ball_x, -Ball_y) %>%
  mutate(
    x = (x - 0.5) * 105,
    y = (y - 0.5) * 68
  )

# Ball data
ball_data <- tracking_home %>%
  select(Period, Frame, time = `Time [s]`, Ball_x, Ball_y) %>%
  mutate(
    Ball_x = (Ball_x - 0.5) * 105,
    Ball_y = (Ball_y - 0.5) * 68
  )
```

## Visualization of Data

```{r}
library(gganimate)
library(sportyR)

# Soccer pitch via sportyR
pitch <- geom_soccer(
  league = "FIFA",
  display_range = "full",
  color_updates = list(
    plot_background      = "#195905",
    defensive_half_pitch = "#195905",
    offensive_half_pitch = "#195905"
  ),
  rotation = 0
)

# Filter to first 30 seconds
example_sequence <- all_players %>%
  filter(Period == 1, time >= 0, time <= 30) %>%
  drop_na(x, y) %>%
  mutate(pt_color = ifelse(team == "Home", "#FF4444", "#4444FF"))

ball_sequence <- ball_data %>%
  filter(Period == 1, time >= 0, time <= 30)

# Extract player number helper
player_num <- function(p) sub("Player", "", p)

# Build animation
anim <- pitch +
  geom_point(
    data  = example_sequence,
    aes(x = x, y = y, color = pt_color),
    size  = 5, alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data  = example_sequence,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = player_num(player)),
    color = "white", size = 2.2, fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data   = ball_sequence,
    aes(x  = Ball_x, y = Ball_y),
    color  = "white", size = 3,
    shape  = 21, fill = "black", stroke = 2
  ) +
  scale_color_identity() +
  transition_time(time) +
  labs(
    title    = "Time: {round(frame_time, 1)}s",
    subtitle = "Red = Home | Blue = Away"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12)
  )

# Render at 25 Hz (real-time speed)
animate(
  anim,
  nframes  = length(unique(example_sequence$Frame)),
  fps      = 25,
  duration = 30,
  width    = 800,
  height   = 600,
  renderer = gifski_renderer()
)
```

## STRAIN Metric

```{r}
PITCH_LENGTH <- 105  # metres
PITCH_WIDTH  <- 68   # metres

# =============================================================================
# Helper: Reshape wide tracking data to long format + rescale
# =============================================================================

parse_tracking <- function(tracking_raw, team_label) {

  # Rename time column
  tracking_raw <- tracking_raw %>% rename(time = `Time [s]`)

  # Ball positions, rescaled
  ball <- tracking_raw %>%
    select(Period, Frame, time, Ball_x, Ball_y) %>%
    mutate(
      Ball_x = (Ball_x - 0.5) * PITCH_LENGTH,
      Ball_y = (Ball_y - 0.5) * PITCH_WIDTH
    )

  # Player positions, long format
  players <- tracking_raw %>%
    select(Period, Frame, time, matches("^Player\\d+_[xy]$")) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = matches("^Player\\d+_[xy]$"),
      names_to = c("player", ".value"),
      names_pattern = "^(Player\\d+)_(x|y)$"
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(x), !is.na(y)) %>%
    mutate(
      team = team_label,                 # keep team as separate column
      x    = (x - 0.5) * PITCH_LENGTH,
      y    = (y - 0.5) * PITCH_WIDTH
    ) %>%
    select(Period, Frame, time, team, player, x, y)  # team separate from player

  list(players = players, ball = ball)
}


# =============================================================================
# Helper: Frame-level possession from event data (LOCF)
# =============================================================================
# Each event covers Start.Frame:End.Frame.
# Ball-carrier = To player if a recipient exists, else From player.
# Gaps between events are filled via last-observation-carried-forward.

build_event_possession <- function(possession_data, all_frames) {

  event_frames <- possession_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(Start.Frame), !is.na(End.Frame)) %>%
    rowwise() %>%
    mutate(Frame = list(seq(Start.Frame, End.Frame))) %>%
    unnest(Frame) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(
      possession_team   = Team,
      possession_player = if_else(!is.na(To) & To != "", To, From)
    ) %>%
    arrange(Frame, desc(Start.Frame)) %>%
    distinct(Frame, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
    select(Frame, possession_team, possession_player)

  all_frames %>%
    select(Frame) %>%
    distinct() %>%
    arrange(Frame) %>%
    left_join(event_frames, by = "Frame") %>%
    tidyr::fill(possession_team, possession_player, .direction = "down") %>%
    tidyr::fill(possession_team, possession_player, .direction = "up") %>%
    mutate(
      possession_source = if_else(
        Frame %in% event_frames$Frame, "event", "interpolated"
      )
    )
}

# =============================================================================
# Main: Calculate STRAIN
# =============================================================================
calculate_soccer_strain <- function(player_data,
                                    ball_data,
                                    possession_data,
                                    goalkeeper = "Player11") {

  # Infer attacking direction (unchanged)
  attacking_direction <- player_data %>%
    filter(player != goalkeeper) %>%
    group_by(Period, team) %>%
    summarise(mean_x = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    group_by(Period) %>%
    mutate(
      away_mean_x        = mean_x[team == "Away"],
      away_attacks_right = away_mean_x < 0,
      attacks_right = case_when(
        team == "Away" ~ away_attacks_right,
        team == "Home" ~ !away_attacks_right,
        TRUE ~ NA
      )
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    select(Period, team, attacks_right)

  print(attacking_direction)

  combined_data <- player_data %>%
    filter(player != goalkeeper) %>%
    inner_join(ball_data, by = c("Period", "Frame", "time")) %>%
    mutate(distance_to_ball = sqrt((x - Ball_x)^2 + (y - Ball_y)^2))

  all_frames <- combined_data %>% distinct(Frame, time, Period)

  possession_by_frame <- build_event_possession(possession_data, all_frames)

  # ── Build frame-level target player (ball carrier or pass recipient) ─────────
  # For PASS events, the target is the "To" player during that event's frame range
  # For all other events, the target is the "From" player
  target_by_frame <- possession_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(Start.Frame), !is.na(End.Frame)) %>%
    rowwise() %>%
    mutate(Frame = list(seq(Start.Frame, End.Frame))) %>%
    unnest(Frame) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(
      target_player = case_when(
        Type == "PASS" & !is.na(To) & To != "" ~ To,   # pass recipient
        TRUE                                   ~ From   # ball carrier
      )
    ) %>%
    arrange(Frame, desc(Start.Frame)) %>%
    distinct(Frame, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
    select(Frame, target_player, possession_team = Team)

  combined_with_possession <- combined_data %>%
    inner_join(
      possession_by_frame %>%
        select(Frame, possession_team, possession_player, possession_source),
      by = "Frame"
    ) %>%
    left_join(
      attacking_direction %>%
        rename(possession_team = team, poss_attacks_right = attacks_right),
      by = c("Period", "possession_team")
    ) %>%
    # Join the target player for each frame
    left_join(target_by_frame %>% select(Frame, target_player), by = "Frame") %>%
    mutate(
      is_defending = (team != possession_team),
      ball_in_defensive_third = case_when(
        poss_attacks_right  ~ Ball_x <= -17.5,
        !poss_attacks_right ~ Ball_x >=  17.5,
        TRUE ~ FALSE
      ),
      player_in_defensive_third = case_when(
        poss_attacks_right  ~ x <= -17.5,
        !poss_attacks_right ~ x >=  17.5,
        TRUE ~ FALSE
      )
    )

  # ── Get position of the target player (attacker with/receiving ball) per frame
  target_positions <- combined_with_possession %>%
    filter(!is.na(target_player)) %>%
    # Match target_player name to player column — note possession_player uses
    # the same naming convention as player (e.g. "Player1")
    filter(player == target_player) %>%
    select(Frame, time, target_x = x, target_y = y)

  # ── Find closest DEFENDER to the target player each frame ────────────────────
  defensive_players <- combined_with_possession %>%
    filter(
      is_defending              == TRUE,
      ball_in_defensive_third   == TRUE,
      player_in_defensive_third == TRUE
    ) %>%
    left_join(target_positions, by = c("Frame", "time")) %>%
    filter(!is.na(target_x)) %>%
    mutate(
      distance_to_target = sqrt((x - target_x)^2 + (y - target_y)^2)
    ) %>%
    group_by(Frame, time) %>%
    arrange(distance_to_target) %>%
    slice(1) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    arrange(time)

  if (nrow(defensive_players) == 0) {
    message("No frames matched — check attacking direction inference")
    return(tibble())
  }

  strain_data <- defensive_players %>%
    arrange(time) %>%
    mutate(
      time_diff       = time - lag(time),
      distance_change = distance_to_target - lag(distance_to_target),
      closure_rate = if_else(
        !is.na(time_diff) &
          time_diff > 0   &
          time_diff <= 0.2 &
          player == lag(player),
        distance_change / time_diff,
        NA_real_
      ),
      distance_safe = pmax(distance_to_target, 0.5),
      strain_raw    = if_else(!is.na(closure_rate), -closure_rate / distance_safe, NA_real_),
      STRAIN        = pmin(pmax(0, strain_raw, na.rm = TRUE), 10)
    ) %>%
    select(
      Frame, time, Period,
      possession_team, possession_player, possession_source,
      target_player,
      ball_in_defensive_third,
      closest_defender = player,
      defending_team   = team,
      Ball_x, Ball_y,
      defender_x  = x,       defender_y  = y,
      target_x,              target_y,
      distance_to_ball = distance_to_target,   # keep same name for downstream compat
      closure_rate, STRAIN
    )

  return(strain_data)
}

# =============================================================================
# Helper: Aggregate STRAIN over rolling time windows
# =============================================================================

aggregate_strain <- function(strain_data, window_seconds = 10) {
  strain_data %>%
    mutate(time_window = floor(time / window_seconds) * window_seconds) %>%
    group_by(time_window, defending_team) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_STRAIN         = mean(STRAIN, na.rm = TRUE),
      max_STRAIN          = max(STRAIN, na.rm = TRUE),
      total_pressure_time = n(),
      mean_distance       = mean(distance_to_ball, na.rm = TRUE),
      mean_closure_rate   = mean(closure_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}
```

```{r}
# Parse tracking data
home_parsed <- parse_tracking(tracking_home, team_label = "Home")
away_parsed <- parse_tracking(tracking_away, team_label = "Away")

strain_players <- bind_rows(home_parsed$players, away_parsed$players)
strain_ball    <- home_parsed$ball   # ball is identical in both files

# Run STRAIN
strain_results <- calculate_soccer_strain(
  player_data     = strain_players,
  ball_data       = strain_ball,
  possession_data = possession_data
)

# Possession source breakdown
strain_results %>% count(closest_defender) 

# Aggregate into 10-second windows
strain_agg <- aggregate_strain(strain_results, window_seconds = 10)
```

## Metric Visualization

```{r}
# STRAIN over time by defending team
ggplot(strain_agg, aes(x = time_window, y = mean_STRAIN, color = defending_team)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.4, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2700, color = "#94a3b8", linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.6) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x     = 2700,
    y     = Inf,
    label = "Halftime",
    color = "#94a3b8",
    size  = 3,
    angle = 0,
    hjust = -0.1,
    vjust = 1.5,
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Home" = "#ef4444", "Away" = "#38bdf8")) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = function(x) paste0(floor(x / 60), ":", sprintf("%02d", x %% 60))
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Defensive STRAIN Over Time",
    subtitle = "Mean STRAIN per 10-second window — attacking third only",
    x        = "Match time (mm:ss)",
    y        = "Mean STRAIN",
    color    = "Defending team"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "mono") +
  theme(
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "#020817", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#0c1628", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#1e293b", linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border     = element_rect(color = "#1e293b", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5),
    plot.title       = element_text(color = "#f8fafc", size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(color = "#475569", size = 9),
    axis.text        = element_text(color = "#64748b"),
    axis.title       = element_text(color = "#475569"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "#0c1628", color = NA),
    legend.text      = element_text(color = "#94a3b8"),
    legend.title     = element_text(color = "#64748b"),
    plot.margin      = margin(20, 24, 16, 20)
  )
```

## Player Rankings

```{r}
# ============================================================================
# Player-level STRAIN summary
# ============================================================================

player_pressure_summary <- strain_results %>%
  group_by(closest_defender, defending_team) %>%
  summarise(
    total_frames      = n(),
    mean_STRAIN       = mean(STRAIN, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_STRAIN        = max(STRAIN, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_distance     = mean(distance_to_ball, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_closure_rate = mean(abs(closure_rate), na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rename(player = closest_defender) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_STRAIN))

print(player_pressure_summary)

# ============================================================================
# Visualization: Average STRAIN per player
# ============================================================================

plot_strain_by_player <- function(player_pressure_summary) {

  team_avg <- mean(player_pressure_summary$mean_STRAIN, na.rm = TRUE)

  plot_data <- player_pressure_summary %>%
    arrange(desc(mean_STRAIN)) %>%
    mutate(
      player      = factor(player, levels = rev(player)),
      strain_norm = (mean_STRAIN - min(mean_STRAIN)) /
                    (max(mean_STRAIN) - min(mean_STRAIN))
    )

  # Top factor level = highest STRAIN player (sits at top of chart)
  top_level <- levels(plot_data$player)[nlevels(plot_data$player)]

  x_max <- max(player_pressure_summary$mean_STRAIN)
  x_step <- if_else(x_max <= 2, 0.5, if_else(x_max <= 5, 1, 2))

  ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = mean_STRAIN, y = player)) +

    geom_vline(xintercept = seq(0, ceiling(x_max), by = x_step), color = "#1e293b", linewidth = 0.4) +

    geom_vline(xintercept = team_avg, color = "#f97316", linewidth = 0.7, linetype = "dashed") +
    # ✅ Use a factor level for y, NOT a number — prevents scale_y_continuous conflict
    annotate(
      "text",
      x     = team_avg + 0.1,
      y     = top_level,
      label = paste0("Team avg: ", round(team_avg, 2)),
      color = "#f97316", size = 3, hjust = 0, vjust = -0.8, family = "mono"
    ) +

    geom_col(aes(fill = strain_norm), width = 0.65, show.legend = FALSE) +

    geom_text(
      aes(label = round(mean_STRAIN, 2)),
      hjust = -0.2, color = "#94a3b8", size = 3.2, family = "mono"
    ) +

    scale_fill_gradientn(colors = c("#38bdf8", "#f97316", "#ef4444"), limits = c(0, 1)) +

    # ✅ No limits= here — use coord_cartesian instead to avoid clipping labels
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, ceiling(x_max), by = x_step), expand = c(0, 0)) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, max(player_pressure_summary$mean_STRAIN) * 1.25)) +

    labs(
      title    = "Average STRAIN Per Player",
      subtitle = "Defensive pressure index (attacking third only) higher = more pressure applied",
      x        = "Mean STRAIN",
      y        = NULL
    ) +

    theme_minimal(base_family = "mono") +
    theme(
      plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "#020817", color = NA),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#0c1628", color = NA),
      panel.grid       = element_blank(),
      panel.border     = element_rect(color = "#1e293b", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.6),
      axis.text.x      = element_text(color = "#475569", size = 9),
      axis.text.y      = element_text(color = "#94a3b8", size = 10),
      axis.title.x     = element_text(color = "#334155", size = 9, margin = margin(t = 10)),
      axis.ticks       = element_blank(),
      plot.title       = element_text(color = "#f8fafc", size = 20, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 6)),
      plot.subtitle    = element_text(color = "#475569", size = 9, margin = margin(b = 20)),
      plot.margin      = margin(24, 32, 24, 24)
    )
}

plot_strain_by_player(player_pressure_summary)
```

## Animated Visualization of Metric

```{r}
library(magick)
library(patchwork)

# ── Parameters ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ANIM_FPS      <- 10          # frames-per-second for both GIFs
ANIM_DURATION <- 3          # seconds of match time to animate
ANIM_PERIOD   <- 1           # which period to visualise
TIME_START    <- 619
TIME_END      <- TIME_START + ANIM_DURATION

# ── 1. Identify the closest-defender player per frame from strain_results ──────
#    strain_results already has closest_defender + Frame + time
closest_def_lookup <- strain_results %>%
  filter(Period == ANIM_PERIOD) %>%
  select(Frame, closest_defender, defending_team)

# ── 2. Player + ball slices for our window ────────────────────────────────────
players_window <- all_players %>%
  filter(Period == ANIM_PERIOD, time >= TIME_START, time <= TIME_END) %>%
  drop_na(x, y) %>%
  left_join(closest_def_lookup, by = "Frame") %>%
  mutate(
    is_closest   = replace_na(player == closest_defender & team == defending_team, FALSE),
    pt_color     = ifelse(team == "Home", "#ef4444", "#38bdf8"),
    # ring colour: gold for closest defender, transparent otherwise
    ring_color   = ifelse(is_closest, "#facc15", NA_character_),
    pt_size      = ifelse(is_closest, 7, 4.5)
  )

ball_window <- ball_data %>%
  filter(Period == ANIM_PERIOD, time >= TIME_START, time <= TIME_END)

# ── 3. STRAIN metrics window (for the right-hand panel) ──────────────────────
#    We use the raw strain_results (frame-level) instead of the 10-s aggregates
metrics_window <- strain_results %>%
  filter(Period == ANIM_PERIOD, time >= TIME_START, time <= TIME_END) %>%
  mutate(
    velocity  = abs(closure_rate),
    frame_idx = Frame - min(Frame)
  ) %>%
  select(Frame, frame_idx, time, distance_to_ball, velocity, STRAIN, defending_team) %>%
  # Right-join to all frames in the window so every frame is represented
  right_join(
    all_players %>%
      filter(Period == ANIM_PERIOD, time >= TIME_START, time <= TIME_END) %>%
      distinct(Frame, time),
    by = c("Frame", "time")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # When Home is NOT the defending team, metrics are meaningless → NA
    distance_to_ball = ifelse(defending_team == "Home" | is.na(defending_team),
                              distance_to_ball, NA_real_),
    velocity         = ifelse(defending_team == "Home" | is.na(defending_team),
                              velocity, NA_real_),
    STRAIN           = ifelse(defending_team == "Home" | is.na(defending_team),
                              STRAIN, NA_real_)
  ) %>%
  arrange(time)

# Shared dark theme for metric plots
dark_theme <- theme_minimal(base_family = "mono") +
  theme(
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "#020817", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#0c1628", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#1e293b", linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border     = element_rect(color = "#1e293b", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text        = element_text(color = "#64748b", size = 7),
    axis.title       = element_text(color = "#475569", size = 8),
    strip.text       = element_text(color = "#94a3b8", size = 8),
    legend.position  = "none",
    plot.margin      = margin(4, 8, 4, 8)
  )

# ── 4. Build LEFT animation (pitch) ──────────────────────────────────────────
anim_pitch <- pitch +
  # normal players
  geom_point(
    data  = players_window,
    aes(x = x, y = y, color = pt_color, size = pt_size),
    alpha = 0.85
  ) +
  # gold highlight ring for closest defender
  geom_point(
    data   = players_window %>% filter(is_closest),
    aes(x  = x, y = y),
    color  = "#facc15", fill = NA,
    shape  = 21, size = 10, stroke = 2
  ) +
  # player number labels
  geom_text(
    data  = players_window,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = sub("Player", "", player)),
    color = "white", size = 2.0, fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  # ball
  geom_point(
    data   = ball_window,
    aes(x  = Ball_x, y = Ball_y),
    color  = "white", size = 3.5,
    shape  = 21, fill = "black", stroke = 2
  ) +
  scale_color_identity() +
  scale_size_identity() +
  transition_time(time) +
  labs(
    title    = "Time: {round(frame_time, 1)}s",
    subtitle = "Red = Home | Blue = Away | ○ = Closest Defender"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 13, face = "bold",
                                 color = "#f8fafc", family = "mono"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 9,
                                 color = "#94a3b8", family = "mono"),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#020817", color = NA)
  )

gif_pitch <- animate(
  anim_pitch,
  nframes  = ANIM_DURATION * ANIM_FPS,
  fps      = ANIM_FPS,
  duration = ANIM_DURATION,
  width    = 600,
  height   = 420,
  renderer = gifski_renderer()
)

# ── 5. Build RIGHT animation (metrics panel) ──────────────────────────────────
#    Three stacked plots: distance / velocity / STRAIN.
#    A vertical line slides with `transition_reveal` using `time` as the reveal var.

# We need one combined tall ggplot with three facets.
# Reshape metrics to long for faceting.
metrics_long <- metrics_window %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(distance_to_ball, velocity, STRAIN),
    names_to  = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    metric = factor(metric,
      levels = c("distance_to_ball", "velocity", "STRAIN"),
      labels = c("Distance (m)", "Velocity (m/s)", "STRAIN")
    )
  )

# Static background ribbon for each facet (the full trace)
metrics_bg <- metrics_long   # full trace, drawn as static grey ribbon

# For the animated "current position" dot + leading trace we use transition_reveal
anim_metrics <- ggplot(metrics_long, aes(x = time, y = value, color = metric)) +
  # full-trace background (light)
  geom_line(
    data  = metrics_bg,
    aes(x = time, y = value),
    color = "#1e3a5f", linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 0.6
  ) +
  # animated growing trace
  geom_line(linewidth = 1, alpha = 0.9) +
  # animated dot at front
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Distance (m)"       = "#38bdf8",
    "Velocity (m/s)" = "#f97316",
    "STRAIN"             = "#ef4444"
  )) +
  facet_wrap(~ metric, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Defensive Metrics - Home",
    x     = "Match time (s)",
    y     = NULL
  ) +
  dark_theme +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "#0f2235", color = NA),
    plot.title       = element_text(color = "#f8fafc", size = 12,
                                    face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 8))
  ) +
  transition_reveal(time)

gif_metrics <- animate(
  anim_metrics,
  nframes  = ANIM_DURATION * ANIM_FPS,
  fps      = ANIM_FPS,
  duration = ANIM_DURATION,
  width    = 420,
  height   = 420,
  renderer = gifski_renderer()
)

# ── 6. Stitch side-by-side with magick ───────────────────────────────────────
tmp_pitch   <- tempfile(fileext = ".gif")
tmp_metrics <- tempfile(fileext = ".gif")

anim_save(tmp_pitch,   animation = gif_pitch)
anim_save(tmp_metrics, animation = gif_metrics)

frames_pitch   <- image_read(tmp_pitch)
frames_metrics <- image_read(tmp_metrics)

n_frames <- min(image_info(frames_pitch)$image %>% nrow(),
                image_info(frames_metrics)$image %>% nrow())
n_frames <- min(nrow(image_info(frames_pitch)), nrow(image_info(frames_metrics)))

combined_frames <- image_join(
  lapply(seq_len(n_frames), function(i) {
    image_append(
      c(frames_pitch[i], frames_metrics[i]),  # single brackets, not double
      stack = FALSE
    )
  })
)

combined_gif <- image_animate(combined_frames, fps = ANIM_FPS)
# Save and display in rendered output
tmp_combined <- tempfile(fileext = ".gif")
image_write(combined_gif, tmp_combined)
knitr::include_graphics(tmp_combined)
```
